mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and drive 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in prolonged rate persistence.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% near threshold, CPIAUCSL at 323.364 elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline by more than 15 basis points in > 50% of triggered 20-day windows, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,300 thousand units, signaling weakening consumer demand. This demand contraction reduces inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth concerns dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 726,283M, HOUST at 1,428K units currently
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise by more than 20 basis points despite trigger activation in > 40% of instances, or correlation between RSAFS-HOUST weakness and yield direction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, indicating labor market deterioration. This signals recession risk, prompting flight-to-safety flows into Treasuries and causing 10-year yields to decline sharply.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE currently at 4.3%, near historical lows
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 60-day windows following UNRATE spike > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1% month-over-month, indicating robust supply-side expansion. This supply strength reduces inflation pressures and supports real yields, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as growth expectations improve without inflation concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.9867 as of 2 months prior
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 50% of 5-day windows during periods of INDPRO acceleration > 1% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth falls below 100K per month, indicating synchronized demand and labor weakness. This dual slowdown triggers expectations of Fed rate cuts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized AND PAYEMS growth < 100K MoM
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise by more than 25 basis points in > 45% of triggered 60-day windows, or the correlation between synchronized GDPC1-PAYEMS weakness and yield direction is not significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling monetary easing. This rate cut cycle reduces discount rates for future cash flows and causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the yield curve steepens.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 20-day windows following FEDFUNDS cuts > 0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K per month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 4.0%, indicating tight labor markets. This labor tightness sustains wage pressures and inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the Fed maintains restrictive policy.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation persistence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline by more than 20 basis points in > 50% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction effect between PAYEMS strength and low UNRATE does not significantly predict yield direction (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) growth turns negative month-over-month, indicating synchronized contraction in housing and consumption demand. This demand collapse signals recession risk and triggers safe-haven flows, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of 5-day windows following synchronized HOUST-RSAFS collapse, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.
